Northern Pacific grade separa-
ton plans were presented to Mayor
Moore and the bridge committee
of the council this morning by Ed
ward J. Cannon, division counsel.
Besides a casual observance of the
plans, the committee did nothing.
The plans will be formally present
ed to the council next Tuesday
night.


1t Is evident that they must be
changed from present specification
If the city is to accede. The COM
pany demands that Brown St, one
OF the best streets in the business
section east of Howard, be closed
altogether. 1t is also proposed to
depress Division st. about eight
feet. Little opposition should de-
velop to this on account of the fact
that although the improvement
might not be as desirable one as
some might wish, 1t is an improve
ment, nevertheless, and will be a
change from the present condition
on that crossing, which has his
tory of blood and accident.


1t will be necessary, if the plans
are to be carried out, to depress
Howard and Mill SITS. also. from
two to three feet. Washington St,
1t is planned to raise about six
feet


Two retaining walls of concrete
filed with sand and rock will ex-
tend through the city as far as
Maple st. except at the crossings,
which will be bridged with steel
Orders. From Maple St west to
the city limits the line will be car
